title: Sweet French Burgundy Toast
description: A sweet and snappy French-inspired dish featuring the warm spices of Ras el hanout, perfect for a monsoon evening. Enjoy as a dessert or snack.
instructions: Slice the French bread into thick pieces and toast until lightly browned.
Mix the coconut sugar, Ras el hanout, cinnamon, and cardamom in a small bowl.
Brush the mixture onto the toasted bread, making sure each piece is evenly coated.
In a separate bowl, mix the maple syrup and coconut cream until smooth.
Drizzle the syrup mixture over the bread, then sprinkle with chopped pistachios and fresh mint.
Season with a pinch of salt to bring out the flavors.
Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 24 hours, perfect for a quick breakfast or snack.
If desired, reheat in the oven at 350°F (180°C) for 5 minutes to revive the flavors.
To make this recipe more substantial, consider adding some sliced banana or avocado on top of the toast.
